description: A horizontal bar chart compares the mean grain size of three Fe-Al ferritic low-density steels against a traditional Al-free interstitial-free (IF) steel. The x-axis is labeled 'Mean grain size (μm)' spanning 0 to 100. The four bars represent, from top to bottom: 9.7Al-0.04C-0.09Ti at approximately 18 μm, 8.1Al-0.0015C-0.14Ti at approximately 90 μm, 6.8Al-0.0035C-0.10Ti at approximately 69 μm, and IF steel at approximately 20 μm. Low-density steels with ultralow carbon and sufficient stabilizer content (6.8 and 8.1 wt.% Al) exhibit dramatically coarser grains than the traditional IF steel. In contrast, the 9.7 wt.% Al steel with higher carbon and insufficient stabilizer shows a finer grain size comparable to or slightly below the IF baseline. This figure demonstrates that grain size in Fe-Al ferritic lightweight steels is strongly governed by carbon level and stabilizing element content, where ultralow C and adequate stabilizer promote significant grain coarsening relative to conventional ferritic grades.
